source.title: BEARS PVE Guide
source.author: BEARS Community
source.dedication: You will be missed but never forgotten my friend

intro: Your time is worth more than the wrecks on the field. Blitzing is about chaining missions and completing them as efficiently as possible, prioritising engagement and momentum over the tangible loot you'd get from looting and salvaging.

Blitzing focuses on speed, flow, and staying in the action. The only exceptions are Burner wrecks and Zor, as they have a chance to drop faction modules or high-value implants that justify the brief pause to loot them.

location_advice.title: Choosing a location
paragraphs: So where is the best location? This is subjective. Some NPC Corporations dictate the area you will run. A primary example is if you want to run lvl 4's for the Sisters of Eve, Llangisi is the only place you can do this in high sec.
Below is a list of potential locations to run missions in high sec but this is by no means an exhaustive list. The criteria was at least 2 mission agents and a truesec rating of around the 0.49 mark.
These are just a suggestion. You should get a feel for the area and corporation you want to run for and move as you see fit.

Shemah - 0.84 Truesec - Home of the B.E.A.R.S Alliance: This is the home of the Bears alliance. That is all the reason you need. Mission with like-minded mission runners around you in a constellation that will never send you to low sec. Located in Kador with access to the Amarr trade hub. (Carthum Conglomerate - LP Store)

Chibi - 0.48 Truesec - Island Home of the B.E.A.R.S Alliance: Chibi is the Island home of the Bears. Located in Aridia, this High Sec island is attached to low sec which deters a lot of undesirable players who may want to liberate you from your PvE hulls. (CBD Corporation - LP Store)

Lanngisi - 0.46 Truesec: Sisters of Eve - LP Store - Considered one of the best mission running locations by many because of the True Sec of the system and the value of the LP store. Very close proximity to the Minmatar Trade hub of Hek. Rife with Gankers however.

Uuhulanen - 0.49 Truesec: Located in Black Rise, 9 jumps from Jita. Has access to two level 4 agents in system with the Caldari Navy and Laidai corporations. Very popular stores with Jita close by. (Caldari Navy LP Store, Lai Dai - LP Store)
Shuria - 0.48 Truesec: Located in Domain, 6 jumps from Amarr. Two MIO agents in the same system. (Ministry of Internal Order - LP Store)
Neesher - 0.52 Truesec: Located in Kor-Azor next door to Low sec. (The Scope - LP Store, Republic Security Services - LP Store)
These are the recommended skill plans for the Anomic Teams portion of the guide which can be found in the In Game Channel, Carebears Den.
Skill plans brought to you by Kaos Kalliope
You can either copy the list from each of the sub sections here or import directly in game.

Implants are a fundamental part of EVE Online. Be it for increasing learning attributes or improving combat performance, a cheap set of implants is a sound investment and will make their cost back several fold over the course of their use.

As you skill up, learn the burner missions and start earning that wonderful ISK, an upgrade path for your implants is recommended. This is based on increasing the repair power of your modules and improving the damage output and application of your weapon system.

For those who are either blitzing missions or using the 'Blazing Burners' concept, consider a Mid-grade Ascendancy set as this will reduce your time in warp and allow you to move from mission to mission faster.

Return on Investment: the fittings have been optimized for cost and effectiveness. The current interpretation of 'Return on Investment' that uses replacement value is considered flawed by the author - each module and hull (sans rigs) has an intrinsic value on resale, retaining about 85% of its initial value, so you need to exceed 15% of the cost to be in true profit.

Boosters and Drugs are a quick and easy way to add an extra edge to your burner running experience. They are one use buffs that have a base 30 minute duration and can be stacked for maximum effects. For the most effective use, Biology should be trained to at least 4, with 5 recommended (extends duration to 1 hour).
Implants have slots 1-10 and boosters have slots 1-20; some share the same slot and only one boost per slot can be active at any one time.

Drugs within EVE Online are one of the most useful buffs available to pilots. They are manufactured by EVE players and sold on the marketplace so supply and cost may vary. While the cost may seem high, over an hour the ISK you can make in the buff duration makes them more palatable.

Each drug also has a grade or strength which defines what drawbacks and how high the chance of a drawback occurring is: Synth (Synthetic) 6% bonus, no side effects; Standard 20% bonus, 20% base chance of side effects; Improved 25% bonus, 30% base chance of side effects; Strong 30% bonus, 40% base chance of side effects. Common side effects include reduced duration, decreases to repair amount or base amount for shields and armor, tracking penalties and reduced missile performance.

Drifter drugs exist but are currently financially unviable due to supply issues and are not part of this guide. In general this guide recommends the use of Synth (Synthetic) drugs to avoid the drawbacks.
This guide does not recommend the use of redeemable event/NES boosters unless their use would allow you to avoid a lost ship.

The Package - recommended drug/booster loadout: Slot 1 - Synth Blue Pill; Slot 2 - Synth Drop; Slot 3 - Synth Crash; Slot 11 - Agency 'Hardshell' TB3 Dose I/II. Additionally, based on supply (replace where duplication): Slot 11 - AIR Hardshell Booster II; Slot 15 - AIR Rapture Booster II.

drugs.name: Blue Pill
drugs.effect: Boosts shield repair amounts but increases signature radius.

drugs.name: Exile
drugs.effect: Boosts armor repair amounts but reduces maximum velocity.

drugs.name: Crash
drugs.effect: Increases explosion velocity/reduces radius for missiles.

categories.name: Anomic Teams
overview: Each sub-section will cover off the builds and tactics for running the Anomic Team set of missions.
Whilst it is possible to lose ships in these missions, they are a great entry point for tackling burner missions.
In terms of investment, it is possible to complete all the team missions using a single hull with a minor refit and loading the specific ammo type as detailed in the guide.

You will need to have Rockets trained to V along with Rocket Specialisation. The build is not cap stable unless you have Target Painting V. In that instance, swap the Peripheral Compact Target Painter for a Parallel Enduring Target Painter.
The initial investment is approximately 124m isk but can vary depending on your primary trade hub.

With the appropriate connection skills, each Anomic team will net you around 5m in mission reward, 5m in Bounty and around 29m in LP value (based on a return of 2100 isk per LP) for a total of 39m. They also offer the chance to loot faction mods. The missions can take around 5 mins to complete including travelling time once you are experienced.

facts.enemies: One assault frigate supported by two logistics frigates
facts.challenge: The logistic ships repair the main target, making it hard to break through
facts.income: ~39m per mission (5m mission reward + 5m bounty + ~29m LP value at 2100 isk/LP)

categories.name: Anomic Agents
overview: Anomic Agents are a ramp up in difficulty but very efficient once you become proficient in completing them.

facts.enemies: Solo pirate frigates (e.g. Daredevil, Succubus, Worm)
facts.challenge: These NPCs use officer/deadspace gear, implants, and player-like tactics.

variants.label: Daredevil
variants.author: bears

quick.note: Pre-activate the webs so they apply as soon as you lock the burner.

instructions: Warp in, lock the Burner Dramiel and keep at range 1700m; it will move into orbit as you close. Pre activate the webs so they apply as soon as you lock the burner.

The Dramiel will always fly towards you to orbit and fire; when it's webbed and its transversal is low, fire the pre-overloaded guns. It's a damage race at this point but assuming good skills you will always win - the Burner should be dead within 20 seconds of starting to fire.

variants.label: Daredevil - The Annoying Agent (advanced)
variants.author: xvassilov

quick.note: Focus on MWD speed being at least 3.7k/s without overheating; faction/deadspace MWD upgrades recommended.

instructions: Agent Guristas proves a serious challenge to a pure trained Nergal pilot. The accepted Nergal fitting requires managing overheating your microwarp drive to catch the Worm, which is not repeatable or reliable. The other accepted answer is a rocket-based shield tanking Garmur, but that requires a considerable cross-train (approx 3 mil SP / 4.8 Bil ISK).

Instead the Daredevil is suggested as an addition to the fleet. Focus on your MWD speed being at least 3.7k/s without overheating. Upgrades to faction or deadspace MWDs are recommended when possible. A faction damage module will also increase effectiveness, as would an upgrade to a faction Small Cap Battery.
There is also a second experimental Daredevil build in testing that uses a 60% Stasis Webifier instead of the Warp Scrambler; the guide will be updated once testing is completed.
For an additional 1.5 mil SP (3b ISK), the Daredevil will allow you to run ALL Agents and Teams with reliable, repeatable, dependable burner running.
The Daredevil is a punching fighting frigate that has outstanding DPS and speed. Matched with a good Kinetic heavy tank it can hunt down and destroy the Worm.

(Void) Land in site, approach burner and turn on MWD. If required apply ONE cycle of overheating to the MWD to make the chase faster. Once within range, apply Warp Scrambler and turn off MWD. Set a Keep at Range of 1500m, then apply guns. Loot.

categories.name: Anomic Bases
overview: The most difficult of the Anomic mission types but repetition is key.

Angel base is arguably the mission that people feel most uncomfortable with initially due to the mid mission refit. After completing this a couple of times, it will become second nature. If this is putting you off attempting the mission then give it a try once. You enter with max cap regen and only refit after the danger is over.
Completing the Guristas Base is not advised due to the length of time it takes to complete the mission. This is not blitzable and thus doesn't fall within the ethos of Blitzing.

variants.label: Vigilant
variants.author: bears

quick.orbit: Orbit hauler at 2500m then keep at range 1500m (post-refit)
quick.note: Mid-mission refit: Mobile Depot swaps the Cap Battery for a 50MN MWD after the Dramiels are down.

instructions: As you drop out of warp ensure your ship stays still and deploy the MTU and Mobile Depot, then deploy the drones for tank assistance. The drones will die, that's ok and why T1 are used. If you forget to bring the drones, overheat your hardeners and your guns but monitor the heat levels for burnout. Never forget the drones.
Target Dramiels and web when in range and repeat until all are dead.

Then using the Mobile Depot refit the Cap battery to the 50MN MWD, scoop all loot/deployables, turn off the reps, turn on the MWD until in range and orbit the hauler at 2500m. Once you are in orbit, switch to keep at range 1500m and fire until destroyed.

This all sounds more complex than it is. The danger is over once the Dramiels are down and you are not under pressure during the refit. (This build requires very high fitting skills; if you are on the newer end of the spectrum, consider replacing the T2 armor reps with C-Type Deadspace modules, est 100m each.)

variants.label: Vigilant - Angel Cartel Base (advanced)
variants.author: xvassilov

quick.orbit: Orbit the Hauler with drones; web then guns on each Dramiel
quick.note: No mid-mission refit needed - the original Mobile Depot-to-MWD refit is redundant; use medium drones on the Hauler instead.

instructions: The Cartel Base is in principle a simple challenge: Four Burner Dramiels and a virtually unarmed Burner Hauler. The reality however is quite deadly. The Dramiels are small (25m Sig Radius), fast (1200m/s), orbit at 3500m and hit like a truck. A nightmare for missile application and a serious challenge for turret tracking. They also web and warp scramble.

Without the use of some very esoteric Abyssal-rolled Stasis Webifiers, the only answer is to use the Vigilant. The Big Brother of the Daredevil, with its webifier strength bonus and solid close range DPS it is the only hull that can reliably clear the Dramiels in a reasonable time.

The original version of the fitting called for the use of a Mobile Depot to refit to a MWD once the Dramiels are killed, however this fit is now redundant. Escort Dramiels aggro on medium drones with a 25% chance. We can use this to use medium drones to kill the hauler.

On landing, drop the MTU and begin locking the Hauler and Dramiels as they burn in. Drop Vespa IIs and engage the Hauler with them. Let the Dramiels slow down and get into orbit then apply webs then 2 seconds later guns. Rinse and repeat through the Dramiels. Scoop loot and MTU.
If the Dramiels swap to the drones (25% chance), recall that drone to the drone bay then relaunch.

variants.label: Vagabond
variants.author: bears

quick.note: This mission has a lower final payout than other burner missions, but the ~15m isk bounty plus faction drop chance still make it worthwhile. RHML can be swapped for a tractor beam for easier loot collection.

instructions: This mission is the anomaly amongst the Burners with a lower final payout than any other burner mission. Still very much worth doing when you factor in the 15m isk bounty the rat provides plus the potential for a faction drop.
Approach and shoot the Sentinel's with Phased Plasma. Swap ammo to Hail to kill Ashimmu.
The RHML can be swapped for a tractor beam for easier loot collection.

variants.label: Vagabond - Blood Raider Base (advanced)
variants.author: xvassilov

quick.note: Total capacitor drain on site is 87.5Gj/s; do not drop below 68Gj/s capacitor excess. Burner Sentinels also apply tracking disruption.

instructions: The Blood Raider base, in good old Blood Raider lore style, is focused around a tanky cruiser with two support frigates that are pure capacitor vampires. The total drain on the site is 87.5Gj/s, and thanks to a change made by CCP to how Capacitor Batteries work we can at least mitigate part of that. It is however recommended not to drop below 68Gj/s capacitor excess.

Added to the neutralization is the usual warp scrambling, webifying and warp disruption. The Burner Sentinels also use tracking disruption, which reduces your tracking speed and optimal range of your turrets.

With all of this going on, and with the need to have high tracking and a strong tank while keeping a high capacitor regeneration rate, there are very few cruiser sized hulls that fit the bill. The favourite, and in the opinion of the guide writer best, is the Vagabond. With a ridiculous shield tank bonus, and the 'mysterious' turret tracking capability, it is the go to hull.

Pre load RF Phased Plasma. On landing on site, drop the MTU and begin locking the Sentinels as they burn in. Once they are locked, lock the Ashimmu. As soon as the Sentinels get a stable orbit, apply turrets and missiles. Rinse and repeat for the second Sentinel. Reload to Hail and apply turrets and missiles to the Ashimmu. Because of the over-tuned tank on the Ashimmu this is the longer part of the fight. Scoop loot and the MTU.
Some guides advise a strategy of approaching the Sentinels to improve tracking, however the author has never had issues just sitting still. Try each method and find which works for you.
warnings: Do not drop below 68Gj/s capacitor excess.

missions.name: Guristas Base
missions.enemy: Waves of Dragonfly fighters + Mantis fighters (from a 'stolen' supercarrier)
missions.caveat: The main guide advises this is not blitzable and takes too long to be worth running under the Blitzing ethos. Xvassilov's advanced section is more blunt: 'Do not run this burner.' It is only worth running once, to complete the full set of 13 burners.

variants.label: Vagabond
variants.author: bears

quick.orbit: Keep at range 4000m on every enemy

instructions: Keep at range 4000m on every enemy.

Enter the mission, target the Dragonflies, once in range, web enemy vessel and start firing when your ship is in line with the target, after a couple of volleys launch drones and repeat until dragonflies are all dead. Ensure you keep moving when the Mantis fighters appear and again keep at range 4000 on the Mantis fighters. Recall drones just before the last mantis dies as the next wave of dragonflies will kill drones fast. Rinse and Repeat for 2 additional waves.

variants.label: Vagabond - Gurista Base (advanced)
variants.author: xvassilov
variants.cost: ~1.5 Bil ISK 'and part of your soul' (Shield Clone)

quick.orbit: Keep at range 4000m on every fighter
quick.note: Author's explicit advice: do not run this burner.

instructions: Do not run this burner. No seriously, do not run this burner.

The Gurista Base is the EVE equivalent of sitting on a tar roof in the middle of summer while covered in oil with no clouds in the sky. The site contains a 'stolen' super carrier which spawns waves of fighters and fighter bombers. Each wave is slow to clear due to small signature sizes and high speeds making tracking and missile application torturous. Three waves of each type, three fighters in each wave.
Set your keep at range to 4000m before entering the site, it will be used on every fighter.

On landing on site drop MTU and lock the first wave of Dragonflies, turn on AB then approach one. Once you are in range, apply webifiers and keep at range 4000m to the Dragonfly. Apply turrets and missiles and once you see shield damage, launch drones and set to attack. After the Dragonfly dies, repeat with the rest.

When all Dragonflies are destroyed, recall drones and orbit the MTU at 15km. The Mantis wave will spawn, using the same process to kill this wave except as they are slower and a little easier to hit. Drones can be put to use once the first Mantis has some shield damage. Once the wave is clear, recall drones and orbit the MTU at 15km.
Repeat this for another four waves (two Dragonfly two Mantis) then watch the Supercarrier jump out.
To complete all 13 burners you will need to run the Gurista Base once. Beyond that achievement, there is no reason to continue running the burner.
warnings: Do not run this burner unless completing the full set of 13 for the achievement.

variants.label: Deimos (cruiser, standard)
variants.author: bears

quick.note: Manual pilot ('Q' key / double-click) to each Talos with MWD and Tank on.

instructions: The mission arena is filled with proximity based turrets. If you get too close to one, you will aggro that turret for the duration of the mission. Aggroing a few is easily tankable in the Deimos but too many can be an issue.

Use manual piloting to navigate the field to clear and engage the Talos. Navigation can be completed by double clicking in space or pressing the 'Q' key as a shortcut and manually drawing a line to a destination in space. Manually fly to Talos 1 with MWD and Tank on.
When in range of a Talos disengage MWD and orbit at 2500 and engage, repeat with Talos 2 and 3.

variants.label: Vagabond - Serpentis Base (advanced)
variants.author: xvassilov

quick.note: Search 'Burner Talos' on Youtube for the manual piloting part of this site.

instructions: Also known as just 'Talos', the Serpentis Base is a test of technical piloting and nerve. Three sniping Talos battlecruisers are spread (170km) around a 'minefield' of auto turrets that can and will shoot if you get within 10km. The sheer incoming alpha that these three ships put out is horrifying.

The standard hull for this content is the venerable Deimos, but the author personally finds that the Vagabond is both faster in terms of going Talos to Talos and can apply high levels of explosive damage. With good piloting, clearing this site in 7 minutes is average. If the guns have problems hitting the target at 5 or 6km orbit, adjust to find the best for your skills.
It is HIGHLY recommended that you search 'Burner Talos' on Youtube and watch one of the many videos that visually explain the manual piloting part of this site.

On landing on site, look towards your right (looking down your ship) and double click in space between the two blue turrets. Once you have aligned, turn on the MWD and buckle up, this is the most dangerous part of the site. If you hit armor during this first burn phase warp out.

Continue burning until you clear the minefield, turn to the left and manually pilot towards the right side of the nearest Talos. Once you are within 65km, set an orbit of 6000m. Turn the MWD off at 35km and lock the Talos when able. Drop drones, apply turrets and missiles and attack with drones. The drones will acquire aggro from the Talosi, hopefully shedding dps. When your target drops to structure, manually pilot towards it and as it dies you should be able to loot the wreck and align towards the edge of the minefield towards the next Talos. Pull drones and MWD on.
Rinse and repeat this process three times, and with the Loot Gods blessing the loot will be Faction Modules.
warnings: If you hit armor during the first burn phase across the minefield, warp out.

per_mission.instructions: Land in site and press + (go faster) icon, Lock, apply webs and disruptor, keep at range 6000m. Loot.

Land in site and orbit Jaguar at 6000m. Lock the Jaguar, apply webs and disruptor. Loot. If you are unable to burn down the Jaguar, lock one of the Burst frigates, load Mystic and shoot it until dead. Keep the orbit and webs on the Jaguar.

notes: This fitting covers both the Agent Angel and Team Jaguar missions. Using short range ammo to overwhelm the target as the spool up DPS burns through.
